text:
The Summer Olympics are always full of inspiring stories, making the quadrennial event must-watch television for 16 days. However, it is also theÂ biggestÂ marketing event in the world for athletes and brands.

Earlier this week, Team USA announced its men's basketball roster for the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, France. Webroke down the rosterby sneaker brands.Â

But we would be lying if we said we were not a little disappointed by the selections based on the player's footwear. So, today, we will reimagine the roster and build the Dream Team (3.0 or 4.0?) based entirely on sneakers. Our selections are below.

There is no shortage of point guards with excellent signature sneaker lines.Â Among the many deserving players,Â we are tapping Stephen Curry to run our offense.Â The veteran guard is on his 11th signature sneaker with the American-based brand Under Armour (and Curry Brand).

Backing up Curry, we are going with two younger point guards with exciting lines that have loyal legions of fans. LaMelo Ball (PUMA) and Trae Young (adidas) are both on their third signature sneaker,Â whichÂ areÂ just as thrilling as theirÂ individualÂ play styles.

Could you imagine the wild colorways PUMA would design for Ball? Meanwhile, fans of the classics would appreciate that adidas has already dropped two colorways of Young's sneakers inspired by the1996 Summer Olympicsin Atlanta, Georgia.

Starter:Stephen CurrySecond String:Trae YoungThird String:LaMelo Ball

At the shooting guard position,Â we agree with Team USA's selection of Anthony Edwards (adidas) and Devin Booker (Nike).Â Of course, they used different criteria in making their choice. Edwards and Booker recently launched their signature sneaker lines,Â and bothÂ are impossible to ignore.

Lastly, we are giving the third string spot to James Harden, whose eighth signature sneaker with adidas is the most futuristic model we have seen on the hardwood in years. Even better, Harden has Olympics experience and is deserving of a swan song on the international stage.

Starter:Anthony EdwardsSecond String:Â Devin BookerThird String:James Harden

With six roster spots already filled, we must become more selectiveÂ at this point. TheÂ easiestÂ choices for the small forward spots are Jayson Tatum (Jordan Brand) and Kawhi Leonard (New Balance).Â TwoÂ signature athletes with very respectable sneaker linesÂ thatÂ would make the Stars and Stripes proud.

Starter:ÂJayson TatumSecond String:ÂKawhi Leonard

ThisÂ is where things getÂ tough. At the four-spot, we go with twoÂ of theÂ more accomplished players in league history. Even better, LeBron James and Kevin Durant (bothÂ Nike athletes) have the longest-running signature sneaker lines among active NBA players.Â

James and Durant can also pull out retro modelsÂ justÂ like they often dive deep into their bags with unbelievable moves on the court. Nike may have botched the uniforms for this year's Team USA basketball team, but it is impossible to mess up James' and Durant's iconic sneakers.

Starter:ÂLeBron JamesSecond String:ÂKevin Durant

Michael Jordan and the Dream Team helped make basketball a global sport during the 1992 Summer Olympics in Barcelona, Spain.Â It is only fitting that the Air Jordan 38 (maybe even the Air Jordan 39Â by then) gets the spotlight.Â So, we give Bam Adebayo (Jordan Brand) the starting center spot.

Joel Embiid only recently signed with Skechers, which was perfect timing for the Los Angeles-based brand. Embiid does not have a signature sneaker, but we are excited to see what Skechers can accomplish on the world's biggest basketball stage.

Starter:Â Bam AdebayoSecond String:Â Joel Embiid

Further Reading:The Nike Kobe 8 Protro "Mambacita" releases on May 1 in full-family sizing.

Pat Benson covers the sneaker industry for Kicks On Sports Illustrated. title: Building USA's Sneaker Dream Team for the 2024 Olympics
description: Rebuilding the 2024 Team USA men's basketball roster based on the best sneakers.
